chapter_context.summary: Prescribes the worship laws for the prince and people on the Sabbath and new moon at the inner gate, details the rules of entry and exit, and describes the temple boiling places for the sacrifices of the people.

outline.text: Ordinances of Prince's Offerings on Sabbaths and New Moons
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ordinances of Prince's Offerings on Sabbaths and New Moons (46:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Rules of Entry and Exit for Worshipping Congregations
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rules of Entry and Exit for Worshipping Congregations (46:9-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Laws Restricting Prince's Land Gift Inheritances
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Laws Restricting Prince's Land Gift Inheritances (46:16-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Boiling Places in Temple Corners for Sacrifices
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Boiling Places in Temple Corners for Sacrifices (46:19-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies of Hope and Restoration
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies of hope and restoration, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 46, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
